description: Interesting scenario: a GDPR-style erasure request — hard_delete=true (row removed, not just marked) with a human-readable reason captured in the audit log. Combines ids and filter for a belt-and-suspenders erasure.
notes: The reason field is required by policy in regulated deployments and lands verbatim on the audit_log row (per ARCHITECTURE.md §3). Hard delete removes the row from memories but the audit_log entry remains — that is the design intent: erase the data, not the proof we erased it.
